question: What's the best time to visit Lakes Entrance VIC with kids?
answer: Spring (September-November) and autumn (March-May) are absolute crackers for visiting with little ones! You'll dodge the summer crowds, enjoy milder weather, and score better rates at holiday parks. That said, summer school holidays (December-January) are magic for beach days and water activities. Just book well ahead as it gets busy. Winter's surprisingly lovely too – quieter, cosy, and perfect for spotting dolphins!

question: Are there pet friendly holiday parks in Lakes Entrance VIC?
answer: Too right there are! Several holiday parks around Lakes Entrance welcome your furry family members. BIG4 Whiters Holiday Village and Lakes Entrance Country Cottages are popular pet-friendly options. Most require pets to be kept on leads, have vaccination records, and stay off furniture. Always ring ahead to confirm their pet policy and any additional fees. Your pooch will love the beach walks!

question: How much do holiday parks cost in Lakes Entrance VIC during school holidays?
answer: School holiday rates typically range from $150-$400 per night depending on accommodation type and park facilities. Powered sites start around $60-$90, while cabins can hit $250-$400+ during peak periods. Easter and Christmas holidays are priciest. Book 6-12 months ahead for school holidays to snag the best spots and rates. Off-peak prices drop significantly – sometimes half the cost!

question: What should I pack for a holiday park stay with young kids?
answer: Beyond the usual clothes and toiletries, pack: beach towels, swimmers, sunscreen (SPF50+), hats, insect repellent, first aid kit, kids' bikes or scooters, outdoor toys, torches for night adventures, and games for rainy days. Most cabins have kitchens, so BYO basics if self-catering. Don't forget comfort items like favourite teddies! Oh, and a portable clothes line is brilliant for drying swimmers.

question: What is there to do near Lakes Entrance VIC holiday parks for families?
answer: You're spoilt for choice! Hit the gorgeous beaches, cruise the Gippsland Lakes, visit Griffiths Sea Shell Museum, feed pelicans at the footbridge (daily 10:30am), explore playgrounds, try mini golf, or walk the Lakes Entrance Boardwalk. Kids love the waterslides at holiday parks too! Nearby you've got Buchan Caves, Lake Tyers Beach, and fantastic bushwalking. There's seriously something for every age!
